headline: ROSS BEATY'S LUMINA METALS RAISES $297M IN UPSIZED TSX IPO — COPPER-SILVER BET ON POLAND
snippet: Lumina Metals Corp. raised C$406M ($297M) in a multiple-times-oversubscribed IPO on the TSX on April 24, giving the company a C$1.3B market cap. The Ross Beaty-chaired company holds three copper-silver projects in southwestern Poland — one of the most significant copper-silver discoveries in Europe in decades. Proceeds fund development toward production.

headline: GOLD DROPS TO $4,685 AS IRAN'S CHIEF NEGOTIATOR QUITS — EXPERTS SEE PLATEAU, NOT BREAKDOWN
snippet: Gold hit $4,685/oz Thursday after Iran's parliament speaker resigned from ceasefire negotiations, citing IRGC interference — extending a ~3% weekly loss. Analysts say the selloff matches prior market-shock patterns, not a structural reversal, with most year-end targets above $5,000. JPMorgan holds its $6,300/oz forecast and calls the miners' 20% GDX pullback a buying opportunity.

headline: VANGUARD MINING REBRANDS AS URANIUM ONE MINING CORP. — EFFECTIVE APRIL 27, 3.5:1 CONSOLIDATION
snippet: Vanguard Mining Corp. (CSE: UUU) will begin trading as Uranium One Mining Corp. on April 27, following a 3.5:1 share consolidation. The rebrand reflects a strategic pivot to its uranium portfolio: the Yuty Prometeo project in Paraguay, and the Nuclean and Quark properties in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin. Post-consolidation shares outstanding: approximately 29.1 million.

headline: WORLD'S NO. 6 URANIUM MINER ONLINE: NAVOIYURAN STARTS QIZILKOK MINE IN UZBEKISTAN
snippet: Uzbekistan's Navoiyuran has commenced commercial ISR uranium production at its Qizilkok deposit — its third-largest mine — after moving from pilot phase in December 2024. Qizilkok holds 9,400t of uranium reserves over a 15-year mine life, with annual output of ~1,200t. Navoiyuran operates 43 uranium deposits globally and is the world's sixth-largest uranium producer.

headline: HOCHSCHILD BEATS Q1 ESTIMATES WITH 75,599 GEOs — REALIZED GOLD PRICE JUMPS TO $4,471/OZ
snippet: Hochschild Mining delivered Q1 2026 production of 75,599 gold-equivalent ounces — ahead of BMO's 69,200 oz estimate — as the realized gold price surged to $4,471/oz from $2,708 a year earlier. The company ended March with ~$412M cash, turning net cash positive. Full-year guidance of 300,000–328,000 GEOs was reaffirmed alongside the Peru and Brazil operations.

headline: BHP LIFTS COPPER GUIDANCE TO NEAR 2M TONNES AFTER RECORD ESCONDIDA THROUGHPUT
snippet: BHP now expects FY26 copper production to land in the upper half of its 1.9–2.0 million tonne range after Escondida — the world's largest copper mine — set a concentrator throughput record. Improved recoveries at Escondida offset lower grades, while Antamina compensated for weaker output at Spence. BHP has also filed permit applications for a new Escondida concentrator.

headline: WHEATON PRECIOUS METALS BACKS SPANISH MOUNTAIN GOLD WITH $55M ROYALTY — BC FEASIBILITY STUDY NEXT
snippet: Spanish Mountain Gold has sold a 1.5% NSR royalty to Wheaton Precious Metals for US$55M, paid in three tranches tied to drilling and permitting milestones at the BC Cariboo project. Proceeds will advance a feasibility study the company aims to complete within 18 months, targeting a construction decision in 2028. Wheaton's backing is a strong signal on project economics at current gold prices.

headline: NEW FOUND GOLD SECURES $205M TO ADVANCE QUEENSWAY TOWARD PHASE 1 — EDGEPOINT AND SPROTT LEAD
snippet: New Found Gold (NFGC) closed a $205M package comprising a $100M bought-deal equity raise (co-led by BMO and SCP Resource Finance) and a $105M credit facility from EdgePoint Investment Group, with cornerstone support from Eric Sprott. Proceeds fund Phase 1 development at Queensway, Newfoundland, and ramp-up of the Hammerdown mine. The offering closes around April 27, 2026.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE'S $3.8B TRIPLE-DEAL TO CONSOLIDATE FINLAND'S CENTRAL LAPLAND GOLD DISTRICT
snippet: Agnico Eagle announced three simultaneous acquisitions to own the Central Lapland Greenstone Belt in northern Finland: Rupert Resources ($2.9B, 67% premium), Aurion Resources ($481M), and B2Gold's 70% Fingold stake ($444M). The total $3.8B package creates a district-scale gold hub in a politically stable, low-risk jurisdiction. All three deals expected to close Q3 2026.

headline: CORE LITHIUM SECURES $290M AND GLENCORE BACKING TO RESTART FINNISS MINE — FIRST ORE IN MAY
snippet: Core Lithium has locked in a $290M funding package — including a $70M convertible note from Glencore's Australian unit and a A$120M equity raise — to restart its Finniss lithium operation in the Northern Territory, shuttered since 2024. Underground mining at BP33 begins May 2026, targeting 134,000t of SC6 spodumene concentrate with first shipment expected in Q4 2026.

headline: PPX MINING PLANS 9,500 METRES OF 2026 DRILLING AT CALLANQUITAS GOLD-SILVER MINE IN PERU
snippet: PPX Mining Corp. (CSE: PPX) outlined its 2026 drill program: 9,500 metres at the Callanquitas East Mine in Peru, targeting high-grade oxide and sulphide zone extensions, with two rigs starting in June. The company also plans exploration drilling at the Portachuelos project. PPX is a low-profile Peruvian gold-silver producer that has been quietly expanding its resource base.
